claim: MLX (Apple's machine learning framework) variants of open models run 1.5-2x faster than GGUF equivalents on Apple Silicon; this is the single biggest performance lever for local LLM inference on Mac hardware.
actionable: Always use MLX-optimized models over GGUF on Apple Silicon; check HuggingFace for mlx-community versions before defaulting to generic GGUF quantizations.
source_title: My M5 Max, Gemma 4, MLX LOCAL Stack. (This KILLS MODEL PROVIDERS)

claim: The M5 MacBook Pro is 15-50% faster than M4 Max for local LLM inference across multiple models and benchmarks, with the advantage being largest in prefill speed (nearly 2x for large contexts).
actionable: If running local models on Mac, upgrade to M5 Max; the 15-50% speed improvement justifies the cost, especially for agentic/long-context work.
source_title: My M5 Max, Gemma 4, MLX LOCAL Stack. (This KILLS MODEL PROVIDERS)

claim: Local model performance degrades sharply at 8K+ token context lengths; at 32K context, models start returning incorrect answers or timing out (30+ seconds per response), making practical context limit ~8-16K tokens despite advertised 160K+ limits.
actionable: For local models on consumer hardware, assume true usable context window is ~8K tokens; anything beyond 16K becomes unusable (>30s latency or accuracy drops).
source_title: My M5 Max, Gemma 4, MLX LOCAL Stack. (This KILLS MODEL PROVIDERS)

claim: Gemma 4 26B MLX variant fits in 16GB RAM and is extremely parameter-efficient ('maximizing intelligence per parameter'), making it competitive with Qwen 3.5 35B while using less memory.
actionable: For resource-constrained local inference, prioritize Gemma 4 26B over larger models; it delivers Sonnet-class performance in a smaller footprint.
source_title: My M5 Max, Gemma 4, MLX LOCAL Stack. (This KILLS MODEL PROVIDERS)

claim: Token decode speed above 30 tokens/second is usable; below 20 tokens/second is the 'dead zone' where latency becomes annoying for interactive use.
actionable: Target 30+ tokens/second as the minimum threshold for user-facing applications; optimize hardware/quantization if you drop below that.
source_title: My M5 Max, Gemma 4, MLX LOCAL Stack. (This KILLS MODEL PROVIDERS)

claim: Qwen 3.5 and Gemma 4 perform similarly on reasoning/accuracy tasks, but Qwen slightly edges out Gemma 4 in breadth-first search and graph traversal benchmarks (both struggle at 32K+ context).
actionable: For reasoning-heavy local work, either Qwen 3.5 or Gemma 4 MLX are comparable; choose based on model size constraints and availability of MLX variants.
source_title: My M5 Max, Gemma 4, MLX LOCAL Stack. (This KILLS MODEL PROVIDERS)

claim: Local models can run inside agentic workflows (Pi coding agent tested) up to ~8-16K context; beyond that, response times become prohibitive (30+ seconds) and accuracy drops.
actionable: Decompose agentic workflows to keep context windows under 8K per sub-task; use local models for micro-agents on simple tasks, reserve cloud APIs for complex multi-turn reasoning.
source_title: My M5 Max, Gemma 4, MLX LOCAL Stack. (This KILLS MODEL PROVIDERS)

claim: For the Pi coding agent with complex multi-tool tasks (package generation with 26+ tool calls), Gemma 4 and Qwen 3.5 MLX both succeed at correctness (~0.7+ scores) but M5 is 3-4x faster than M4 (7-14s vs 40-60s).
actionable: Local agentic coding is viable on M5; for simple/mid-complexity tasks, local models save API costs. For production at scale, cloud APIs still outperform.
source_title: My M5 Max, Gemma 4, MLX LOCAL Stack. (This KILLS MODEL PROVIDERS)

claim: NVFP4 (Nvidia's 4-bit floating point format) provides good compression on Qwen 3.5 but isn't the primary driver of MLX's speed advantage on Apple Silicon—MLX framework optimization matters more.
actionable: Don't assume NVFP4 is necessary for local speed; focus on finding MLX-native models over NVFP4 GGUF when choosing quantizations for Apple hardware.
source_title: My M5 Max, Gemma 4, MLX LOCAL Stack. (This KILLS MODEL PROVIDERS)

claim: By end of 2026, consumer hardware should be able to run Sonnet or Opus 4.0-class models locally if current hardware trends and model compression improvements continue.
actionable: Plan for local LLM deployment in products; assume consumer-grade hardware will support frontier models by end of year. Start building local inference stacks now.
source_title: My M5 Max, Gemma 4, MLX LOCAL Stack. (This KILLS MODEL PROVIDERS)
